data.title: Analysts largely positive after Intel's Q2 results but mull valuation, Foundry business
data.description:
Intel's (INTC) second-quarter results and guidance beat estimates. However, Seeking Alpha analysts highlighted low expectations making the "print look like a home run," and the Foundry segment, which "is still not really a well-working business."

Meanwhile, Wall Street analysts were largely positive about the company but highlighted “rich” valuation and thoughts around data center CPU share.

Shares of Intel (INTC) fell about 3% on Friday.

SA Analysts

"Back in early June, I downgraded Intel (INTC) to a sell following Nvidia (NVDA) penetrating the consumer PC processor market with the RTX Spark superchip. Part of what justified my sell at the time was that the stock was overvalued. What I’ve seen since then is the stock reset, dropping about 26% over the past month alone," said Seeking Alpha analyst The Techie.

"I think this helped create a setup with low enough expectations that is making this print look like a home run. The results are good, but I’ve seen better results get punished this quarter. I think the market was looking for a positive to price in here, as the guide is better than expected, though the company still printed a sequential decline and soft margin expansion," said the analyst.

Seeking Alpha analyst Louis Gerard said "Intel has just posted a relatively strong quarter with revenue coming in at $16.1B, up 25% Y/Y and roughly above 12% of the consensus with non-GAAP EPS of 42 cents nearly doubling the consensus thanks to a surge of 59% in DCAI [Data Center and AI Group]."

However, the analyst noted that "the Foundry segment is still not really a well-working business as, yes it posted a revenue base of $5.77B, which is up 31% Y/Y but it still posted an operating loss of -$2.09B and with the intersegment eliminations were set to -$5.48B so external foundry revenue only stood at a mere $300MM. And to be fair, the whole bull thesis behind INTC is about 18A external traction which has not been reflected in the earnings."

Gerard added that the $11B GAAP loss, which is not an impairment but an escrowed-shares derivative as the bridge is a $12.5B mark-to-market charge on shares held in escrow for the Commerce Department under the CHIPS Secure Enclave arrangement.

"Though it does not impact the overall operation it is worth noting that the higher INTC trades, the large the charge will be as the government’s claim appreciates with the equity," Gerard noted.

Wall Street Analysts

BofA reiterated its Buy rating and $160 price objective on Intel's stock.

"Intel’s (INTC) Q2 beat/raise earnings call reconfirms the two pillars of our Buy thesis: 1) Equity External manufacturing (foundry) customer discussions have reached a more tangible stage, leading to capex boost (positive semicaps), and 2) INTC's core server CPU business is strongly participating in the ongoing agentic cycle, with data center sales surging 59% YoY, INTC's best growth rate in the past 15 years. We reiterate Buy and continue to view INTC's US-based leading-edge capacity and strong backing by the White House as long-term competitive moats," said analysts led by Vivek Arya.

The analysts noted that risk-wise we must watch for any capital market action to fund the capex, though they noted that Intel has other levers, like non-core asset disposals, plus potential customer prepayments as alternatives.

Arya and his team added that external foundry customers will start their engagements this year and into 2027, with larger revenues more in 2028-30, leading to a few years of high capex and low free cash flow, or FCF.

Needham kept its Hold rating on Intel's stock with no price target.

"Intel reported a beat and raise, as all segments exceeded expectations, led by DCAI despite tight supply," said analysts led by N. Quinn Bolton.

However, the analysts noted that "despite the turnaround underway, we remain Hold rated as we believe Intel is losing share in DC [data center] CPUs, the company is not competitive in Al accelerators and valuation is rich."

Wedbush kept its Neutral rating but increased the price target to $98 from $60.

"Intel handily exceeded prior guidance. And the company's new forecast, despite outpacing prior consensus, also looks extremely beatable given strong underlying conditions (and in particular higher CPU PX). Net, we expect fundamentals to remain positive for the world's largest producer of compute. At the same time, we still struggle to justify Intel's valuation (particularly relative to its peers) and thus remain on the sidelines with regard to the stock," said analyst Matt Bryson.

For AMD (AMD), Intel's results were a positive. Bryson said they expect AMD to be able to better take advantage of lifting average selling prices, or ASPs, and a shift towards higher-margin server products, with the analyst's conversations indicating that over the course of the second quarter, AMD has outperformed Intel in supplying incremental product.

For Memory makers the results were positive too. The analyst noted that more server CPUs require more Registered Dual In-Line Memory Modules, or RDIMMs, and more storage, among other things.

In addition, the results were positive for semiconductor capital equipment makers. Bryson said that Intel, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and Micron Technology (MU) have now all increased spending plans.

Top chip equipment makers include ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), and KLA (KLAC).

"Intel continues to benefit from agentic AI CPU demand, which is supporting healthy Xeon demand and improving factory utilization. Improving 18A manufacturing yield remains key to unlocking foundry margins and earnings upside. The stock is trading on 14A, where maturity, yield, and performance are ahead of 18A at the same stage. Multiple external customers are in PDK evaluation. To us, additional catalysts may include external 14A commitments and Google TPU engagement," said BNP Paribas analyst Karl Ackerman.

Update: The story was updated with a BNP analyst note.

data.title: Financials, tech and healthcare lead list of overbought large caps
data.description:
Large-cap stocks are in focus as second-quarter earnings season shifts into high gear, with investors assessing whether recent rallies across financials, healthcare, technology and industrials can be sustained.

Several widely followed companies have posted strong gains in recent weeks, pushing their Relative Strength Index to elevated levels ahead of a busy slate of earnings releases and management guidance.

Associates First Capital Corporation (ASFZ) and Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC) top the list with perfect RSI readings of 100, reflecting exceptional recent price momentum. NFiniTi Inc. (NFTN) follows closely with an RSI of 99, while Apogee Therapeutics, Inc. (APGE) and The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) round out the top five with RSIs of 80 and 78, respectively.

Other notable names include Electronic Arts Inc. (EA), Medpace Holdings, Inc. (MEDP), PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), CSX Corporation (CSX) and Bio-Techne Corporation (TECH), each posting RSI readings of 75 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index is a widely used momentum indicator that measures the pace and magnitude of recent price movements on a scale of 0 to 100. Readings above 70 typically suggest a stock has entered overbought territory following a sustained rally, while readings below 30 indicate oversold conditions.

While elevated RSI levels do not necessarily signal an imminent reversal, investors often monitor these stocks closely during earnings season, when quarterly results and forward guidance can either reinforce bullish sentiment or trigger profit-taking.

Here is the list:

  1. Associates First Capital Corporation (ASFZ), RSI: 100

  2. Wilson Bank Holding Company (WBHC), RSI: 100

  3. NFiniTi Inc. (NFTN), RSI: 99

  4. Apogee Therapeutics, Inc. (APGE), RSI: 80

  5. The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV), RSI: 78

  6. Electronic Arts Inc. (EA), RSI: 77

  7. Medpace Holdings, Inc. (MEDP), RSI: 75

  8. PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL), RSI: 75

  9. CSX Corporation (CSX), RSI: 75

  10. Bio-Techne Corporation (TECH), RSI: 75

data.title: Booz Allen surges after easily beating Q1 earnings, maintaining guidance
data.description:
Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) up 14.9% in early trading Friday after reporting lower but better-than-expected FQ1 adjusted earnings while reaffirming its earnings and revenue forecast for the full year.

Q1 net income fell to $198M, or $1.63/share, from $271M, or $2.16/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenue fell 4.2% Y/Y to $2.8B; adjusted earnings came in at $1.81/share, well above the $1.48 FactSet consensus estimate, adjusted EBITDA rose 7.4% Y/Y to $334M, and adjusted EBITDA margin climbed to 11.9% from 10.6% in the prior-year period.

Booz Allen (BAH) said demand is accelerating across its national security portfolio, causing the company to focus on advanced cyber and defense technologies, as well as AI-native products, while its civil business remains challenged.

The company's backlog totaled $39.48B as of June 30, up 3.2% Y/Y, while total headcount stood at ~30.9K, down 7.5% Y/Y.

CEO Horacio Rozanski said the company is on track to meet full-year guidance for earnings of $6.00-$6.35/share, in line with the $6.26 FactSet analyst consensus, adjusted EBITDA of $1.24B-$1.29B, in line with $1.26B consensus, and revenue of $11.2B-$11.7B, in line with $11.42B consensus.

data.title: New home sales growth in June tops consensus
data.description:
June U.S. New Home Sales: +1.6% M/M to 628K vs. 613K consensus and 618K prior (revised from 580K), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Ipsen phase 3 Bylvay trial in biliary atresia misses primary goal
data.description:
• A phase 3 trial examining Ipsen's (IPSEY) Bylvay (odevixibat) to treat biliary atresia, a rare pediatric liver disease, missed its primary endpoint.
  • The drug failed to show improvement in native liver survival vs. placebo.
  • Bylvay, a once-daily selective and potent ileal bile acid transport inhibitor, is approved for itching associated with Alagille syndrome and progressive familial intrhepatic cholestasis.

data.title: Populism, robots, and gold: How 2020s themes have inverted from the prior decade
data.description:
Bank of America’s global research team has outlined a thoughtful realignment in investment themes, framing the 2020s as an era of inflationary pressures that invert many of the defining forces of the prior decade.

Where the 2010s emphasized demand-side dynamics, globalization, open borders, and U.S. exceptionalism, the current decade prioritizes supply constraints, national security, border controls, and a broader global rebalancing. Political populism has intensified, shifting from earlier forms of populist capitalism toward what the financial institution describes as populist socialism. Central bank independence has given way to greater deference, while quantitative easing has been replaced by tools such as yield curve control. 

At the same time, fiscal excesses of the previous period have evolved into heavy capital expenditure on artificial intelligence. Market leadership is moving away from the Magnificent Seven and private equity toward micro-caps and public equity. 

Demographic and technological transitions are equally stark: Boomers and human labor are yielding to Gen Z and robotics, while the “invisible hand” of markets faces a more visible policy fist. Even the longstanding dominance of the U.S. dollar (DXY) is being challenged by renewed interest in gold (XAUUSD:CUR). 

Collectively, these shifts signal a more fragmented, security-conscious, and inflation-prone investment landscape that investors must navigate carefully.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).
